mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage and retrieval of vector embeddings. It is suitable for applications requiring fast similarity search and filtering capabilities. Key trade-offs include the need for careful configuration to optimize performance and resource usage.

steps.title: Add Qdrant Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Qdrant Helm chart repository to your local Helm setup, allowing you to install Qdrant using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add qdrant https://qdrant.github.io/qdrant-helm

helm repo update

```

> This adds the official Qdrant Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Qdrant CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Qdrant to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qdrant/qdrant/main/deploy/crds/qdrant.io_qdrants.yaml

```

> This command installs the necessary CRDs for Qdrant.

steps.title: Deploy Qdrant using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant application in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm, specifying the desired namespace and version.

```bash

helm install qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --create-namespace --version 1.17.0

```

> This deploys Qdrant in the 'qdrant' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Qdrant Collection
steps.description:
This step creates an example collection in Qdrant to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X POST 'http://localhost:6333/collections/my_collection' -d '{"vectors": {"size": 128, "distance": "Cosine"}}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'

```

> This creates a collection named 'my_collection'.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X POST 'http://localhost:6333/collections/my_collection' -d '{"vectors": {"size": 128, "distance": "Cosine"}}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'

steps.title: Verify Qdrant Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Qdrant pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> You should see the Qdrant pod in a 'Running' state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Qdrant deployment and associated resources from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall qdrant --namespace qdrant

kubectl delete namespace qdrant

```

> This command uninstalls the Qdrant release.
> This command deletes the namespace and any remaining resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step will upgrade your Qdrant installation to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections/my_collection' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'

helm upgrade qdrant qdrant/qdrant --namespace qdrant --version 1.17.0

kubectl get pods -n qdrant

```

> Backup your data by exporting it.
> This command upgrades Qdrant to the specified version.
> Verify that the Qdrant pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X GET 'http://localhost:6333/collections/my_collection' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'

troubleshooting.title: Error: '404 Not Found' when accessing Qdrant API
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be running or the pod may not be ready.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable' when querying Qdrant
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be reachable due to network issues or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly configured and check the network policies.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Invalid configuration' in logs
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant configuration may have invalid parameters.

**Fix:**
Review the configuration file and ensure all parameters are correctly set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused' when trying to connect to Qdrant
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant pod may not be running or the service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status and service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector distance metrics and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API endpoints for collection management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your API calls to use the new endpoints as specified in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Qdrant pod running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n qdrant' showing the pod in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: kubectl exec -it $(kubectl get pods -n qdrant -l app=qdrant -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -n qdrant -- curl -X POST 'http://localhost:6333/collections/my_collection' -d '{"vectors": {"size": 128, "distance": "Cosine"}}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
